title: Smalley and Springer Lead Zurich Classic as Fitzpatricks Chase in New Orleans
excerpt: Alex Smalley and Hayden Springer hold a one-shot lead at the PGA Tour's only team event in New Orleans after two rounds, with the Fitzpatrick brothers surging into contention.
body:
Team Golf in the Bayou

The 2026 Zurich Classic of New Orleans continued Friday with the second round of alternate shot (foursomes) action at TPC Louisiana. After 36 holes, Alex Smalley and Hayden Springer command the leaderboard at 16 under par — a commanding one-shot lead as the weekend approaches. The pair had tied the tournament record with a first-round 14-under 58 in four-ball play, and have maintained their momentum through the format switch.

Leaders Score
-16
Tournament Format
Team (2-Man)
Cut Line
-10
Teams in Field
35 Made Cut

Fitzpatrick Brothers Surge

England's Matt and Alex Fitzpatrick, competing together as a pairing, made a spectacular push up the leaderboard on Friday. The brothers chipped in for an eagle on the 18th hole during their opening nine and carded a team score of 15 under for 36 holes, putting them in a three-way tie for second place. Matt Fitzpatrick won last week's RBC Heritage as an individual, and the brothers' chemistry is evident — Alex noted before the week that they don't see each other as often as they'd like, but when they do play together, they pick up each other's best habits.

Big Names Miss the Cut

In a significant shake-up, defending champions Andrew Novak and Ben Griffin made the cut on the number at 10 under, while two of the tournament's most recognizable stars didn't make the weekend at all. Shane Lowry and Brooks Koepka both failed to advance, a rarity for players of their stature. The cut also sent home Tony Finau and Max Greyserman, leaving 35 teams to contest the final two rounds.

Clutch Performances Light the Way

Nick Dunlap and Gordon Sargent posted a strong 2-under 70 on Friday to reach 14 under, with Dunlap noting that alternate shot format tests patience and partnership in equal measure. "Even if you are really close with your partner, there's still part of you that doesn't want to let him down," Dunlap said. Eric Cole and Hank Lebioda also sit at 14 under despite a closing double-bogey, proving that consistent play trumps dramatic moments in foursomes.

Saturday and Sunday Await

The PGA Tour's only team event continues this weekend at TPC Louisiana, with Saturday's final round of foursomes setting up a Sunday finale of four-ball. Smalley and Springer have shown they can excel in both formats — a rarity at the Zurich. The Fitzpatricks will aim to cap a brilliant week together, while the chasing pack hunts for one final miracle.
